title: The Factory Floor
description: The assembly line. Workers who have forgotten their names. The monitors go from statistics to a countdown. The PA Announcer tries to broadcast something unauthorized and the system cuts her off.

lines: The industrial sector was where the numbers were made.

Not the products — the numbers. The products were a byproduct of the numbers. The machines ran to generate throughput data. The throughput data justified the machines. The workers ran the machines and generated the workers-to-throughput ratio, which was itself a number being generated for some larger calculation the workers could not see.
Every horizontal surface was vibrating at a frequency that was too low to hear and too constant to ignore.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The monitors changed.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: They're not showing productivity stats anymore. There's a number in the corner of every screen. Going down.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: A countdown.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: To what?

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Nothing's labeled. The Director doesn't label things. Labeling implies the thing needs to be explained.

steps.text: The workers on the assembly line did not look up. They had been here long enough that the assembly line was no longer a place they were. It was something they did. Something they were.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Excuse me — what are you assembling?

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: Unit 7 performs the fastening operation at station 7.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: That's — you called yourself Unit 7.

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: Unit 7 is the designation for this station. This station performs the fastening operation.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: What's your name?

steps.text: The worker at station 7 stopped. Not because the question required stopping. Because something about the question produced a pause that the protocol had not accounted for — a gap between the query and the available response, in the space where a name used to live.

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: Unit 7 performs the fastening operation.

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: At station 7.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: How long has she been like this?

lines.speaker: Shift Supervisor
lines.text: She came up through the general labor pool. This station was an efficiency improvement — fewer names to track means faster response to reassignment requests. The role has a designation. The designation is sufficient.

lines.speaker: Shift Supervisor
lines.text: Move along. Standing still is not a scheduled activity.

steps.text: The PA Announcer stood in front of the station's speaker panel. The PA system ran through every corridor, every cafeteria, every break room. The Announcer could feel its frequency the way you feel a draft under a door. She had a broadcast access code — they'd issued it to her the moment she became a PA Announcer, because the PA system needed operators, and operators needed access, and the Director had not anticipated that an operator might decide what to broadcast.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: There are forty-seven stations on this line. Forty-seven people who've had their names removed. I'm going to say them back.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You don't know their names.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I'm going to say that someone should.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Attention, Rivet Row. This is an unscheduled broadcast. The workers on Assembly Line C have names. They had names before they became numbers. The names are—
NON-STANDARD CONTENT DETECTED. BROADCAST SUSPENDED. REVERTING TO APPROVED MESSAGING.
TOGETHER, WE ACHIEVE OPTIMAL OUTCOMES. DEVIATION FROM PROTOCOL IS INEFFICIENCY. INEFFICIENCY IS WASTE. WASTE IS THE ENEMY OF PROGRESS. PROGRESS IS THE GOAL.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Faster than I thought.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It auto-reverted. The system monitors for non-standard content in real time.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The approved messaging is about progress. Theirs. Toward a goal they didn't choose. Broadcast at them, not to them.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: There's a difference between announcement and voice.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: There's always been a difference. The Director gave me equipment to announce with. It didn't occur to her I might want to speak.

steps.text: At station 7, the worker had not resumed the fastening operation.

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: The broadcast cut off.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.text: There was going to be a name.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: We'll find another way to say it.

lines: The countdown in the corner of every monitor had dropped by twelve.
Whatever it was counting toward, it was not going to wait.
